title: XVII
html:
XVII

Crack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝso this is Paris! quoth I (continuing in the same mood)п»ҝвҖ”and this is Paris!п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝhumph!п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝParis! cried I, repeating the name the third timeп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

The first, the finest, the most brilliantп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

The streets however are nasty.

But it looks, I suppose, better than it smellsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝwhat a fuss thou makest!п»ҝвҖ”as if it concerned the good people to be informed, that a man with pale face and clad in black, had the honour to be driven into Paris at nine oвҖҷclock at night, by a postilion in a tawny yellow jerkin, turned up with red calamancoп»ҝвҖ”crack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crackп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝcrack, crack,п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝI wish thy whipп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝBut вҖҷtis the spirit of thy nation; so crackп»ҝвҖ”crack on.

Ha!п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝand no one gives the wall!п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝbut in the School of Urbanity herself, if the walls are besh-tп»ҝвҖ”how can you do otherwise?

And prithee when do they light the lamps? What?п»ҝвҖ”never in the summer months!п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝHo! вҖҷtis the time of sallads.п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝO rare! sallad and soupп»ҝвҖ”soup and salladп»ҝвҖ”sallad and soup, encoreп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”вҖҷTis too much for sinners.

Now I cannot bear the barbarity of it; how can that unconscionable coachman talk so much bawdy to that lean horse? donвҖҷt you see, friend, the streets are so villainously narrow, that there is not room in all Paris to turn a wheelbarrow? In the grandest city of the whole world, it would not have been amiss, if they had been left a thought wider; nay, were it only so much in every single street, as that a man might know (was it only for satisfaction) on which side of it he was walking.

Oneп»ҝвҖ”twoп»ҝвҖ”threeп»ҝвҖ”fourп»ҝвҖ”fiveп»ҝвҖ”sixп»ҝвҖ”sevenп»ҝвҖ”eightп»ҝвҖ”nineп»ҝвҖ”ten.п»ҝвҖ”Ten cookвҖҷs shops! and twice the number of barbers! and all within three minutes driving! one would think that all the cooks in the world, on some great merry-meeting with the barbers, by joint consent had saidп»ҝвҖ”Come, let us all go live at Paris: the French love good eatingп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝthey are all gourmandsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝwe shall rank high; if their god is their bellyп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝtheir cooks must be gentlemen: and forasmuch as the periwig maketh the man, and the periwig-maker maketh the periwigп»ҝвҖ”ergo, would the barbers say, we shall rank higher stillп»ҝвҖ”we shall be above you allп»ҝвҖ”we shall be Capitouls30 at leastп»ҝвҖ”pardi! we shall all wear swordsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”And so, one would swear (that is, by candle light,п»ҝвҖ”but there is no depending upon it) they continue to do, to this day.
